drafts.draft: Hardest part of running an AI agent for an SMB is not the model. Its when a customer asks the owner something, owner answers off-script, and the agent invents a different answer the next day.
drafts.reason: Conversation-starter on real SMB agent failure mode. Bait for replies from agent builders.

drafts.draft: A plumber I set up last week told me he ignored 11 enquiries on holiday because his phone was in airplane mode. The agent caught all 11. Two booked. He didnt know AI worked while he slept.
drafts.reason: Concrete SMB story with numbers. Conversion proof that maps to Automatyn ICP.

drafts.draft: If your agent has a system prompt longer than 800 words, you do not have an agent. You have a wishlist your model is politely ignoring.
drafts.reason: Mild contrarian take. Will pull engagement from anyone shipping bloated prompts.

drafts.target_text: Which one do you prefer building first?    - Audience   - Product
drafts.draft: Audience first if you can stomach posting into the void for 12 months. Product first if you have one specific person who already begged you to build it. Which camp are you in?
drafts.reason: Bait reply-back: hands the binary back as a question.

drafts.target_text: Over the past 25 years we've gone from: Cloud Computing to Claude Computing
drafts.draft: Cloud to Claude is cute but the real shift is from infra you rent to workforce you rent. When does the hubspot side start charging per agent-hour instead of per seat?
drafts.reason: Direct question to Hubspot CTO about pricing model. High reply-back probability.

drafts.draft: The hard part is that micro-decisions feel low stakes in the moment, so you have no signal until 3 years later. Whats your trick for spotting a bad one before it compounds?
drafts.reason: Asks Hiten for his personal heuristic. Personal-question replies convert.

drafts.target_text: Most people use Claude like Google. Thats the mistake. The real power starts when you stop chatting and start...
drafts.draft: Whats the one shift that flipped it for you? For me it was treating the chat like a workspace not a search bar, but Im curious if you mean something more specific.
drafts.reason: Smaller account but on-topic. Asks for her specific framing.

drafts.target_text: Elon Musk called Anthropic evil. Then he signed a deal with them. This is not hypocrisy. This is the...
drafts.draft: Saying its evil is a recruiting moat. Calling it evil while signing the deal is a price negotiation tactic. The actual question is which framing investors believed when reading the contract.
drafts.reason: Mild contrarian. Reframes the take with a sharper angle, baits Shawn to clarify which camp he is in.
